def: s.f. Harlotry, prostitution; fornication, adultery
v.t. = ćhinwānā, q.v.; — also = ćhīnnā, q.v.
s.m. Seizing, seizure.
s.f. = ćhināl, q.v.
s.m. = ćhinālā, q.v.
v.t. To water; to throw out water.
s.m. & adv. See s.v. ćhin.
s.f. The little finger.

ety: S. छिक्का; or verb. n. fr. ćhīṅknā
def: s.f. Sneezing; a sneeze

s.m. A network of strings or cords to hang anything in (as food, pots, a burthen at either end of a yoke or pole, &c.); the cords of a bahangī, q.v.; a rope-bridge across a hill-torrent, or a rope with a sling or basket attached (by which travellers are drawn across); — a muzzle (for cattle).
s.f. Causing to sneeze; sneezing.
v.t. To cause to stop; to cause to seize, to have (one) arrested, &c. ( = ćhiṅkwānā); to score or cross out, to cancel ( = ćheknā)
v.t. To cause to sneeze ( = ćhiṅkwānā).
s.m. The acacia (syn. kīkaṛ; babūl).
v.n. = ćhīṅknā, q.v.

ety: ćhīṅk˚ = Prk. छिक्क(इ) or छिक्के(इ) = S. छिक्कय(ति), fr. छिक्का
def: v.n. To sneeze.
v.t. To cause to be crossed out, or cancelled; to have (one) stopped or detained, &c.; see ćheknā.
v.t. To cause to sneeze ( = ćhiṅkānā, q.v.).
s.f. A harlot; — the tree Menispermum glabrum.
v.n. See چهننا ćhinnā.
v.t. See چهيننا ćhīnnā.

ety: ćhīn˚ = Prk. छिन्न(इ) or छिन्ने(इ), fr. S. छिन्न p.p.p. of rt. छिद्
def: v.t. To pull, snatch, pluck, tear, wrench, wrest, rob, take by force; to seize, usurp
subs: v.t. (intens.), To snatch up; to tear or snatch away; to seize, usurp, take forcible possession of, to deprive (of, -se), to bereave (of), &c.

def: part. adj. Cut, divided, torn, broken, rent, perforated, destroyed
subs: ćhinn-bhinn, ćhin-bhin, adj. Pierced through and through, cut up, broken in pieces, shattered, shivered, destroyed, scattered
s.f. The plant Clypea hernandifolia or Cissampelos hexandra.

def: v.n. To be snatched, to be seized, be wrested (from)
adj. See s.v. ćhinna, ćhinn.
s.m. = ćhinalā, q.v.
s.f. A spot, speck, stain, blot; spattering, splash; a sprinkling or a drop (of water); — chintz; — balance, remainder, odd days (of a month)
subs: s.f. Wages for odd days or for a broken period.

def: s.m. Shot, spot, splash, &c. ( = ćhīṅṭ); a sprinkle, a slight shower (of rain); bespattering, aspersion, ridicule, raillery, banter; — scattering, sowing broadcast; seed scattered at the extremities of a village (with a view to obtain possession); — sowing an inferior crop (as pease, or linseed, &c.) between the lines of the chief crop (e.g. rice, &c.); a field so sown; — a broad shallow basket (used in sowing broadcast, &c.); — a lump or ball (of tobacco or opium); — stimulus, excitement; — deception, trick, toils
subs: ćhīṅṭā mārnā, v.t. To sprinkle, to splash, bespatter; — to deceive, trick; — to encourage, incite, stimulate.

def: s.m. Sowing broadcast ( = ćhīṅṭā).

ety: ćhīṅṭ˚ = Prk. छिट्टे(इ) or छिट्ट(इ), fr. छिट्ट = S. स्पृष्ट p.p.p. rt. स्पृश्
def: v.t. To sprinkle; to scatter ( = ćhiṭkānā and ćhiṛaknā).
adj. & s.m. (in comp.), Snatching, stealing; — snatcher, &c.
v.t. To cause to be snatched; to make one snatch; to get (a thing) snatched away, &c.

s.f. Discoloration or spot (on the skin), white spots (on the skin), leprosy;-a fishing-rod; a leathern strap for buckling on the saddle; — the act of a buffalo's pushing with his horns; — an inferior kind of palm-fruit.

ety: perf. part. of ćhipnā or ćhupnā
def: part. adj. Hidden, concealed, close, secret
subs: (or ćhupā-) rasta, s.m. A secret path, a private road
(or ćhupā) rahnā, v.n. To lie perdu, to hide, be in hiding; to abscond; to be hidden or veiled (from, -par).

def: adv. Secretly, stealthily, unperceived.
adv. Secretly, stealthily, unperceived.

ety: caus. of ćhipnā or ćhupnā; cf. ćhapānā
def: v.t. To cause to be concealed, to conceal, hide, secrete, to cover, to veil
subs: v.t. To hide, conceal, secrete; to keep secret; to abstract; to keep back

def: s.m. Hiding, lurking, concealment, secrecy; hiding-place.
v.t. = ćhipānā, q.v.
s.f. Clandestine action of any kind (as cultivation of land, &c.).

s.m. A cloth-printer, one who stamps floor-cloth, &c. (syn. ćhaperā); — (ćhīp˚+Prk. iʼā = S. ikā), s.f. A fly-flap (a piece of wood with a cloth or a piece of leather attached to it); a small flag waved to scare pigeons, or to make them fly.
s.m. Sprinkling.
s.f. The common house-lizard (of India), a lizard.
adv. = ćhipke, q.v.
adv. Secretly, stealthily, unperceived
s.f. A lizard ( = ćhipkalī, q.v.).

ety: ćhīp˚ = Prk. छिप्प(इ) = S. क्षिप्य(ति). rt क्षिप्; or S. स्पृश्य(ते), pass. (used) actively) of rt. स्पृश्
def: v.t. To print (cloth); — to push with the horns (as a buffalo); — to draw up the rod (in fishing).

ety: ćhip˚ = Prk. छुप्प(इ), or छिप्प(इ) = S. क्षोप्य(ते) caus. pass. of rt. क्षि
def: v.n. To be hidden or concealed; to be absent; to hide, to lurk; to disappear, to set (as the sun, &c.); to put on a veil or mask; to elude or escape observation
subs: v.n. To become hid; to hide, &c. ( = ćhipnā); to retire, to withdraw
v.n. To lie or remain concealed, to hide, &c.

def: adj. (f. -ī), Hiding, lurking, &c.
subs: (or ćhupte) phirnā, v.n. To go skulking about, to be in hiding.

def: s.f. A chip; a shaving; a splinter
s.m. Milk (cf. P. shīr)
subs: s.m. (Myth.) The ocean of milk; (Geog.) the White Sea.

def: s.m. = ćhīr, q.v.; — s.f. = ćhīr, ćhīṭ, and ćheṛ, qq.v.
s.f. The lessoe's own cultivation.
v.t. = ćhiṛaknā, q.v.

ety: ćhiṛak˚ = Prk. छिडक्क(इ) or छिडक्के(इ), fr. S. स्पृष्ट+कृ
def: v.t. To sprinkle, to asperse, to water by sprinkling; — to scatter, strew
subs: v.t. To sprinkle water on stale or faded vegetables or fruits and sell them as fresh; — to puff off goods.

def: v.t. To cause to be sprinkled, to have or get sprinkled, &c.
s.f. = ćherī, q.v.
s.f. A narrow lane or passage; a small private door.
s.m. vulg. corr. of sirkā, q.v.
v.t. To cause (one) to sprinkle; to cause to be sprinkled, have or get sprinkled (by; = ćhiṛakwānā.
s.m. Sprinkling, watering
v.n. To be touched, have the fingers passed over; to be played on (as a stringed instrument); to be molested, be plagued; to be excited, be vexed; to take offence; to fret ( = ćiṛnā, q.v.).
v.t. To cause to be touched, &c. (see ćheṛnā).
s.f. = ćhiti, q.v.
s.f. = H چهيٿا छीटा ćhīṭā, s.m. = ćhīṅṭ and ćhīṅṭā, q.v.
s.m. = H چهيٿ छीट ćhīṭ, s.f. = ćhīṅṭ and ćhīṅṭā, q.v.

ety: ćhiṭak˚ = Prk. छिटक्के(इ) or छिटक्क(इ), fr. S. क्षिप्त+कृ
def: v.n. To be spattered, be splashed, to spurt; to be strewed, be scattered, be dispersed, be dissipated; to be spread, to spread, to be diffused (as light), be shed, to shine, be bright; to be disordered, be deranged, be dishevelled (the hair); to be displaced, be dislocated; — to fly back with a spring (as a bow, the bolt of a door or lock, &c.)
s.m. A brogue, shoe.
adj. Scattered, dispersed.
adj. Scattered, dispersed.
v.n. To be scattered, be strewed, be spread.
s.f. The earth; the soil of the earth
subs: adj. Covering the earth; prostrate on the ground; scattered, dispersed

def: s.m. = ćhīṅṭā, q.v.

v.t. To sprinkle, squirt, splash; spatter; to strew, scatter, disperse, dissipate; to disarrange, to dishevel (hair); to displace, to dislocate; to cause to fly back with a spring (as a bow, or the bolt of a door, &c.).
s.f. Bolt (of a door).
s.f. Splash; speck, spot ( = ćhīṭā); small shot ( = ćharrā)
subs: (-ko), To spot, speck, flecker, to mark with strokes or touches

def: v.n. To be broken, be split, be pounded, be bruised; to be hammered, be flattened; to be beaten, be punished severely.
v.t. = ćhīṅṭnā, q.v.
s.f. A small basket without a cover or handle; a broken basket.

ety: ćhit˚ = S. क्षिप्त p.p.p. of क्षिप्; rā, caus. aff.
def: v.t. To scatter, to strew, to sow broadcast; to spread.
s.m. Scattering, dispersion
s.f. = ćhitnī, q.v.
s.m. Scattering (seed), sowing broadcast.
v.t. To cause to be broken, &c.; to get or have (a thing) beaten, &c.

r: ćhīʼūl, ćhiyūl, ćhiʼūl, Butea frondosa, palās, ḍhāk
def: s.m.(?), The tree Butea frondosa (syn. palās; ḍhāk).
s.m. = ćheʼuli, q.v.
adj. Forty-six.
Ninety-six (sometimes written ćhiyānweṅ, &c.).
adj. Sixty-six.
Eighty-six.
s.m. = ćhoh, q.v.
s.m. Treacle ( = ćoṭā).
s.m. Agitation, excitement, emotion, distress, anguish (cf. ćhoh).
s.m. Agitation, excitement, emotion, distress, anguish (cf. ćhoh).

ety: S. शुश्रूः (fr. desid. of rt. श्रु)
def: s.f. A nurse; — bosom
s.m. Agitation, emotion; love, affection, tenderness; — anger, passion
subs: v.n. To feel anger, &c., to be angry.

def: adj. Affectionate; — angry.
s.m. = H چهوهري छोहरी ćhohrī, s.f. = ćhokrā and ćhokrī, qq.v.
s.f. = H چهوهرا छोहरा ćhohrā, s.m. = ćhokrā and ćhokrī, qq.v.
s.m. A boy, lad; a slave boy; — a dancing man dressed in woman's clothes.
prop. چهوکرا ćhokrā, q.v.

ety: the preceding with इका for कः
def: s.f. A girl, a lass; a slave-girl; a dancing girl.
s.m. A chip; shaving.
s.m. Green gram or chick-pea in the pod; — a labourer who strips off the leaves and lops off the heads of sugar-cane before it is pressed.
s.m. = ćholā, q.v.
v.t. To cause to be cut, clipped, lopped, or pared, &c.; to have (a thing) scraped, &c.
s.f. A kind of small tent (used for sepoys, servants, &c.).

ety: ćhol˚ = Prk. छोल्ल˚; prob. fr. S. त्वष्ट, p.p.p. of rt. त्वक्ष्
def: v.t. To cut, to lop; to skin, peel, pare, scrape, erase (syn. ćhīlnā).
s.f. An instrument for scraping, a scraper.
v.t. = ćholānā, q.v.
s.m. = ćhoʼā, q.v.
s.m. = ćoćlā, q.v.
s.f. The earth
subs: ćhonip, s.m. 'Lord of the earth,' a king, monarch.

def: s.m. See s.v. ćhoni, ćhonī.
s.f. Seasoning, relish; taste, flavour; spices for seasoning food ( = baghār).
s.m. A coat (of paint, &c.); plastering, repairing; filling up
subs: s.m. Covering with plaster or paint, &c., plastering, &c. ( = ćhop)

ety: ćhop˚ = Prk. छुप्प(इ) = S स्पृश्य(ते), pass. (used actively) of rt. स्पृश्; cf. ćhīpnā
def: v.t. = ćhopnā.
s.m. Letting go, loosing, setting free, release, liberation, deliverance; sparing; leaving, abandoning, &c.; relinquishment, renunciation; abdication; omitting, omission
subs: s.f. A pass, a permit; a deed of release; a deed of divorce or abandonment (of a wife); a document relinquishing claim to a girl betrothed to the repudiator, but who, in his absence, is married to another man.

def: s.f. Border, edge, margin, bank, shore; limit, boundary; side, end, extremity, tip, point, summit, brow (of a hill); — a tow-boat.
part. & adv. (gov. the acc.) Having left, leaving, &c.; — omitting, excepting, except, save, besides; setting aside; not to speak of
subs: 'To leave and sit down'; to abandon entirely, give up altogether; to withdraw from
To leave behind, to abandon, forsake, &c.; see ćhoṛnā
To leave at one's death, to die and leave, to bequeath.

ety: Prk. छावडओ; S. शाव+र+कः; cf. ćhokṛā
def: s.m. A boy, lad
subs: Boy and girl; — a children's game ( = beṭā-beṭī).

def: s.m. Letting go, loosing, setting free, release, liberation, deliverance; sparing; leaving, abandoning, &c.; relinquishment, renunciation; abdication; omitting, omission
subs: s.f. A pass, a permit; a deed of release; a deed of divorce or abandonment (of a wife); a document relinquishing claim to a girl betrothed to the repudiator, but who, in his absence, is married to another man.

def: s.m. Abandoning, leaving (see ćhoṛnā).
v.t. = ćhuṛānā, q.v.
s.m. = ćhuṛāʼo, ćhuṛāwā, ćhorā, qq.v.
v.t. = ćhoṛānā, ćhuṛānā, q.v.
s.f. Ransom, &c. ( = ćhuṛautī, q.v.).
s.m. = ćhuṛāʼo, ćhuṛāwā, ćhorā, qq.v.
s.f. A girl, a lass ( = ćhokrī).
s.f. Seeds of Cleome viscosa.
part. (of choṛnā) & adv. = ćhoṛ, q.v.
part. (of choṛnā) & adv. = ćhoṛ, q.v.
v.t. = ćhoṛna, and ćahornā, qq.v.

ety: ćhoṛ˚ = Prk. छोड(इ) or छोडे(इ) = S. छुटय(ति), or (Bhū type) छोट(ति) rt. छुट्
def: v.t. To let go, let loose, to loose; to set free, to free, release, liberate; to spare, forgive, pardon; to let off, discharge; to leave or let alone; to leave, quit; to forsake, abandon, desert; to relinquish, resign, renounce, give up; to abdicate; to abstain from; to omit, to except; to put or lay aside; to discharge, shoot, fire (an arrow, or a gun, &c.); to emit, to give out, pour forth; to cause to run or flow, to pour (water, &c. into a vessel)

ety: ćhūt; or Prk. छुत्ता = S. छुप्ता, rt. छुप्
def: s.f. Touch, defilement, &c.; — malignant influence (esp. on the eyes); — resemblance, likeness; copy, duplicate
subs: (-kī), To use a charm as a remedy for disease of the eye (attributed to malignant influence).

def: adj. (prov. & poet.) = ćhoṭā, q.v.
adj. (f. -ī), Little, small; less, lesser; younger, junior, minor; subordinate, inferior, common; trivial, trifling, unimportant, of no consequence; low, mean, insignincant, contemptible
subs: s.f. A trifling matter or affair, a small or easy matter

adj. & s.m. Great and small, high and low; young and old; varied, various; — ćhoṭā ćānd, s.m. (local), The climbing plant Ophioxylon serpentinum (the root of which is said to be an excellent remedy for snake-bites)
s.m. An assistant to a district officer; a subordinate European officer
'Big words from a little mouth,' proud words from a weak stomach.

def: s.f. = H چهوٿاپن छोटापन ćhoṭā-pan, s.m. See ćhuṭāpā, ćhuṭpan and ćhuṭāʼī.
s.m. = H چهوٿائي छोटाई ćhoṭāʼī, s.f. See ćhuṭāpā, ćhuṭpan and ćhuṭāʼī.
s.m. Skin; rind, peel ( = ćhilkā); — a poppy; opium.

ety: S. क्षुत्, rt. क्षु; or onomat.
def: s.f. Blowing or breathing sound (as made by a washerman beating clothes); blowing or breathing after a prayer or incantation; — intj. used to excite a dog on to his prey
subs: s.f. The blowing or breathing sound made by a washerman beating clothes on a stone to clean them
s.m. Blowing or breathing after an incantation, conjuration, enchantment, magic
(-par), To pronounce an incantation and blow (upon), &c. ( = ćhū-mantar karnā, q.v.)
s.m. An incantation, a charm; — intj. Hey presto! — ćhū-mantar karnā, v.n. To pronounce an incantation and blow (upon), to conjure, to exorcise
v.n. To vanish, disappear suddenly, be off ( = uṛān-ćhū honā).

def: s.f. = aćhwānī, q.v.
s.m. A dried date ( = ćhuhārā).
s.m. = ćhūćhak, q.v.
adj. Empty, void, hollow; vain, light; small, little; thin; trifling; low, mean, insignificant, poor, worthless (cf. ćhićhorā; ćhūćhā; ćhūćhak).
s.f. = ćhūćhī, q.v.
adj. Empty, &c. ( = ćhūćhā); — s.f. The refuse left after extracting the juice from sugar-cane, &c.; refuse.
adj. (f. -ī), Empty, hollow; shallow; low, mean, contemptible, despicable.
s.m. See s.v. ćhū.

s.m. A ceremony observed after childbirth (when the mother visits her father — generally forty days after the birth of her child — and returns with presents of clothes, money, &c.); the presents made on the occasion of the ceremony.
s.f. A piece of a hollow reed or bamboo; a pipe, a tube, a canula; a needle-case; an ear without grain.
v.t. = ćhućkārnā, q.v.
adj. (f. ī), Empty, &c. ( = ćhūćhā, q.v.); foolish, silly, stupid.
s.f. The play of a dog or cat with game, &c.
s.f. The musk rat, Sorex cœrulescens; — a squib; — a rover, a gad-about
subs: To let off a squib; — to backbite, to calumniate; to excite resentment, to cause a quarrel, to set by the ears.

def: s.m. = H چهچهندري छुछुंदरी ćhućhundarī, s.f. = ćhaćhūṅdar, q.v.
s.f. = H چهچهندر छुछुंदर ćhućhundar, s.m. = ćhaćhūṅdar, q.v.
v.t. To breathe an incantation over, to conjure, to exorcise.
v.t. To cheep to (a dog, &c.); to set on or encourage (a dog); — to drive away contemptuously.
s.f. Hunger.
adj. Little, trifling; mean, low, &c. ( = ćhićhorā, q.v.).
v.t. To whitewash.
s.m. A dried date.
s.f. Touching, touch.
v.t. (dialec.) = ćhūnā, q.v.
s.f. Chalk (cf. ćhuhānā).

ety: perf. part. of ćhūnā and mūnā
def: s.f. The sensitive plant, Mimosa sensitiva, or M. pudica; — a sensitive or irritable person; a delicate or frail thing.
s.f. A gnat.

ety: ćhulak˚ = Prk. छुलक्के(इ), fr. S. उद्+श्वल् ( = शल्)+कृ
def: v.n. To pass urine involuntarily (through fear or otherwise); to make water slowly (as a mare, &c. in heat); to discharge semen (cf. ćhulćhulānā).
v.t. To cause to touch, to make (one) touch or feel ( = ćhuwānā).
adj. Trifling, finical; boyish, childish, puerile; — weak in the genital organs or in the bladder, passing semen or urine involuntarily or quickly (through weakness, or fear, &c.).
adj. Boyish, childish, puerile, silly ( = ćhulćhulā, ćhulohlā).
adj. (f. -ī), Trifling; childish, &c.( = ćhullū, ćhulćhulā, qq.v.).
adj. (f. -ī) = ćhulohlā, q.v.

ety: ćhū˚ = Prk. छुव(इ) = S. छुप(ति), rt. छुप्
def: s.m. Touching, touch, contact.
s.f. A needle-case, &c.; — a large needle, a bodkin; — a small cup (for children).
s.f. = چهنگليا ćhuṅgliyā, q.v.
s.f. The little finger.
s.m. A large knife; a dagger; a razor
subs: s.f. & adv. Fighting with knives or daggers, snick and snee; — a quarrel; at daggers drawn.

def: s.m. See چهرا ćhurā.
s.f. Liberation, release, &c. ( = ćhuṛāʼo, q.v.).
v.t. = ćhuṛānā, q.v.

ety: caus. of ćhoṛnā = ćhuṛā˚ = ćhuṛāw˚ = ćhoṛāw = Prk. छोडावे(इ), where āwe = S. आपि, caus. augment
def: v.t. To cause to free or release, &c.; to have (one) released or liberated, &c. ( = ćhuṛwānā); — to set free, to free, liberate, deliver, release; to rescue, extricate; to redeem (a pledge); to dismiss, discharge, remove, get rid of; to separate, to except, to put aside
subs: v.t. (intens.), To set free, &c. ( = ćhuṛānā); — to take or snatch away (from, -se), to rob (of).

def: s.f. A razor-case, razor bag or bundle; tool-bag or bundle.

ety: ćhuṛā(nā)+āʼo = Prk. एअव्वं and एअव्वअं = S. (इ)तव्यं and (इ)तव्य+कं
def: s.m. Deliverer, saviour, &c.

ety: ćhuṛāw(nā) = ćhuṛā(nā)+Prk. तिआ = S. aff. ति+इका
def: s.f. Ransom.

ety: ćhuṛā(nā)+āʼo = Prk. एअव्वं and एअव्वअं = S. (इ)तव्यं and (इ)तव्य+कं
def: s.m. Deliverer, saviour, &c.
v.t. = ćhuṛānā, q.v.
s.f. A knife; a dagger; a scalpel
subs: 'To take breath under the knife'; to be patient in the midst of difficulties, &c.
s.f. Fighting with knives, snick and snee; — daggers-drawn, deadly enemies
s.f. 'A sweet knife'; cold steel; — one who, with a smooth face, effects the ruin of another; a secret enemy.

def: s.f. = ćhurī, q.v.
s.f. A nostril.
v.t. To cause to be released, or let go, &c.; to have (one) released, get (one) his liberty, &c.

ety: ćhū(nā)+Prk. अत्ती = S. आपि+तिः (आप्ति, āpi being the caus. augment
def: s.f. Touch of anything impure, contamination; defilement, impurity
subs: v.n. To be touched, be contaminated, be defiled, be polluted.

def: adj. Little, small, &c. (used in comp.)
subs: s.m. Poor, low, or common people

def: prep. (governing the formative), Omitting, excepting, save, but, without (e.g. tujh ćhuṭ, 'excepting thee'; — syn. ćhoṛ).

s.f. Severance, separation; divorce; liberation, deliverance, release; leaving; relinquishment; freedom, liberty; escape; exemption (from, -se); remission (of revenue, &c.); exception; rejection; leavings, refuse; grain left on the threshing floor; — radiance or splendour (of jewels, &c.); reflection (of a mirror, &c.); — a catch-word; — (in fencing) striking off hand or playing loose, striking the opponent's body anywhere one pleases; — giving money off hand or at will to jugglers, dancing girls, &c., at entertainments; — a bet; letting pigeons fly on a bet that they return to their cot; — adv. Omitting, excepting; apart, aside, &c. ( = ćhuṭ, q.v.)
subs: s.m. Severance, separation, parting; intervention, mediation
v.n. To fight loosely, to hit all round; — to let (the tongue) loose.

def: adj. (f. -ī), Touching
adj. (f. -ī), Defiled by touching, unclean (ceremonially), impure, foul, corrupt; polluted (applied to drinking vessels, dishes, &c.).
s.f. Rescue, deliverance; escape ( = ćhuṛāʼī).
s.f. Littleness, smallness ( = ćhoṭāʼī, q.v.).
adj. = ćhuṭkā; — adv. = ćhuṭ and ćhūṭ, qq.v.
s.f. Leisure, time.
v.t. To cause to be separated; to have or get (one) released or set free, &c. ( = ćhuṛānā, q.v.).
s.f. = ćhuṭān, q.v.
s.m. Littleness, diminutiveness (cf. ćhuṭ and ćhuṭhan).
s.m. (dialec.) = ćhuṛāwā, q.v.
s.f. Remission of revenue or rent.
s.f. See چهٿوتي ćhuṭautī.
adj. dial. var. of the next, q.v.
adj. (f. -ī), Little, small, diminutive; lesser; younger, junior; — the little or lesser one; the younger one.
v.t. To release, set at liberty, &c. ( = ćhuṛānā, q.v.).
adj. & s.m. = ćhuṭkā, q.v.
v.n. To be released, &c. ( = ćhuṭkārā pānā).
s.m. Deliverance, release, &c. ( = ćhuṭ-kārā).
v.n. To be released, &c. ( = ćhuṭkārā pānā).
v.n. To be released, &c. ( = ćhuṭkārā pānā).
adj. (f. -ī) & s.m. Dissolute; — a dissolute man, a rake ( = luććā).
s.f. Dissoluteness.

ety: ćhuṭ˚ = Prk. छुट्ट(इ) = S. छुट्य(ते), pass. of rt. छुट्
def: v.n. To be set free, be liberated, be discharged, be acquitted; to be redeemed (a pledge); to be let go; to get loose; to be loose; to be dishevelled (the hair); to escape (from); to slip (from); to be adrift; to be got rid of, to be separated (from); to be left, be abandoned; to be left out, be omitted; to be left off, be given up, be relinquished; to be let off, be discharged, to go off (a gun); to be free with (the hands), use (the hands) freely (in striking); to cease, stop; to issue, to come out or forth (as blood, life, &c.), to spout, to shoot forth; to come out or off (as colour from cloth, &c.); to begin to move, be set in motion (as sand, or a train, &c.), to start

ety: ćhūṭ˚ = Prk. छुट्ट(इ) = S. छुट्य(ते), rt. छुट्
def: v.n. = ćhuṭnā, q.v.
s.m. Childhood, infancy ( = ćhoṭā-pan).
s.m. Childhood, infancy ( = ćhoṭā-pan).

ety: for ćhuṭā, perf. part. of ćhuṭnā
def: adj. (f. -ī), Separate, detached, single; bare, mere, simple.

ety: ćhuṭ(nā)+tī = Prk. अत्तिआ = S. आपि+ति+का (आप्तिका, āpi being the caus. increment
def: s.f. Dismissal, discharge; release, acquittal, &c. ( = ćhuṭkārā); permission; intermission, cessation; leisure, time; leave, holiday, furlough
subs: (-ko), To give leave, or furlough (to); to dismiss; to discharge, to release
To obtain permission; to obtain leave, get a holiday; to be discharged, be acquitted.

def: v.t. To cause to be set free; to release, &c. ( = ćhuṛānā, q.v.).
v.t. To cause to touch, to make (one) touch, get (one) to touch.
s.m. Resemblance, likeness.

ety: Zend ći, nom. ćis; S. ća
def: conj. What? — which, what; as, because; either, whether, or, even (when prefixed to another word the mute h is dropped, e.g. ći-gūna, q.v.: it is also used as an affix; e.g. agar-ći, 'if even,' 'although'; ān-ći, 'that which'; har-ći, 'everything which,' 'whatever').
s.m. Land which remains long moist.
adj. = next, q.v.

ety: Prk. चव्वइल्लओ; S. चर्व+इल+कः+कः; cf. ćabāʼolā
def: adj. (f. -ī), Boyish childish, puerile; unpolite, rude, uncouth.
s.m. Childishness, puerility
s.f. The chin.
s.f. Matter or fllth in the inner corner of the eye ( = ćīpṛā).
s.m. = ćićṛī, q.v.
s.m. A kind of gourd (used as a vegetable), Bela vulgaris, or Trichosanthes anguina.
s.m. = ćićinḍā, q.v.

ety: S. चित् or चीत् redupl.+ā = āw = Prk. आवे = S. आपि caus. augment
def: v.n. To squeak, squeal, scream, shriek, bleat.
s.m. = ćićinḍā, q.v.
s.m. (f. -ī) = ćhaćhūṅdar, q.v.
s.m. = ćićṛī, q.v.
s.f. A line, a score ( = ćaćīr)
s.f. A tick or louse (of dogs or sheep, &c.)
subs: (-meṅ), To stick (to one, &c.) like a tick.

def: s.m. The tike or tick ( = ćićṛī, q.v.).
s.m. (f. -ī) = ćhaćhūṅdar, q.v.

ety: perf. part. of ćīdan, and = S. ćita, rt. ći
def: part. adj. Gathered, picked, culled; select, choice.
s.m. See s.v. ćit, ćitt.
adv. How? of what kind? in what manner? in what state?
s.f. Nature or manner (of a thing); circumstance; matter; quality.
s.f. A place for cremation ( = ćihānī, q.v.).
adj. Forty
subs: s.f. lit. 'Forty-folds'; a coat of mail ( = ćilta, q.v.)
s.m. A pavilion or palace, &c. supported by forty (or a great number of) columns; also = ćihal-manār, q.v.
s.f. A walk, ramble; — a custom at the funeral of Musalmāns, of stepping back forty paces from the grave and again advancing to it before reading the service over
s.m. 'The forty towers'; a name of the ruined city of Persepolis.

def: v.t. To embellish, decorate.
s.f. A place of cremation, a ghāṭ where corpses are burnt ( = ćihāʼī).
s.m. Name of a subdivision of the Gūjār tribe.
s.m. = ćinh, q.v.
part. adj. See ćinhit.
s.f. = ćeʼolī, q.v.
s.f. A hanging screen or blind (it is made of of split bamboos, or of reeds, placed horizontally at intervals, and strung together); syn. ćilwan.
s.f. A pain in the loins or back.
s.m. (local), A mutton-butcher.
s.f. = ćiq, q.v.
s.m. A cheque, a money-order.
s.f. = ćīkaṛ, q.v. & cf. kīć.
s.m. A musk rat; a mouse.
v.t. To crush, triturate, bruise; to chew, masticate; to press, to squeeze.
s.m.f. A particular mode of working flowers on muslin or other cloth; embroidery, needlework; embroidered cloth
subs: s.m. A worker of flowers on muslin, &c., an embroiderer

def: adj. & s.m. Smooth, sleek, &c. ( = ćiknā); spruce, foppish; — a fop, beau, spark, dandy, swell.
s.f. Mud, mire, slime; land that has been recently irrigated.
s.f. Mud, mire, slime; land that has been recently irrigated.
s.m. The ravine deer (found commonly on the banks of the Jamnā; it is so called on account of the sneezing sound (ćhikkā) it makes when approached by man).
s.m. A kind of fiddle with four or five wires and horse-hair for strings.
adj. Thick-set, sturdy, robust, strong, tough.
s.m. The ravine deer ( = ćikārā, q.v.).
(ći+kāra), adj. lit. 'Of what use'; useless, worthless, contemptible.
s.f. A gnat, a musquito; — smut, obscenity (in this sense prob. ćīk+āla+ikā).
s.f. (local), A hunting-knife.
adj. = ćikāra, q.v.
v.n. To scream, squeak, squeal.
s.f. The greasy and dirty deposit of a lamp; a mixture of oil and dust or dirt; — clayey soil ( = ćīkṭī); — adj. Greasy and black or dirty (as cloth), &c. (see ćikkaṭ).
v.n. To stick together (the hair, &c., through viscosity and dirt), to be matted or clotted; to be sticky, be clammy; to be dirty or filthy
subs: ćikaṭ-rahnā, v.n. intens. of and = ćikaṭnā.

def: s.f. = ćiknāwaṭ, q.v.
s.f. Cry, scream, &c. ( = ćīḵẖ, q.v.).
s.f. Taste ( = svād).
s.f. Scream, screech, shriek, yell
subs: v.n. To raise a cry, to scream, &c. ( = ćīḵẖnā).

def: s.m.(?) The husk of chick-pea or ćanā (used as fodder for cattle).
v.n. To cry out, scream, shriek, roar.
v.t. To taste ( = ćaḵẖnā, q.v.).
v.n. To cry out, scream, &c. ( = ćīḵẖnā, q.v.).
v.t. To taste ( = ćīḵẖnā, ćaḵẖnā, q.v.).

ety: ćīkh+ur = Prk. उल्लो = S. उलः; — but cf. S. चिकुरः
def: s.m. A squirrel.
s.m. A weed.
s.f. A squirrel.
v.t. To weed.
s.f. Wages for weeding.
v.t. To cause to weed, to have or get weeded.
v.t. To cause to scream, to make (one) cry out or shriek, &c.
s.m. Mud, mire ( = ćīkaṛ; kīćaṛ).
s.f. The practice or science of medicine, administering or applying remedies, healing, curing; attendance of a physician on a patient.
s.m. Medical practitioner, physician, doctor.
s.f. (cf. ćik, ćak), A musk rat; a mouse.
